[the volume v of a sphere of radius r is given by the formula v r   4 3 3   ] (4)   (ii)  the depth of water in a cave is being monitored.     the rate of increase in the depth of water, h cm, at a particular point in the cave is  modelled by the differential equation d d h t k h = 3     where k is a constant and t hours is the time after monitoring began.     given that    initially the depth of water was 4 cm    5 hours after monitoring began, the depth of water was 6 cm    t hours after monitoring began, the depth of water was 10 cm     solve the differential equation to find the value of t.     give your answer to one decimal place.
